steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Find the sum of the two acute angles in a right triangle
steps.content:
In any triangle, the sum of all three angles is 180°. A right triangle has one angle measuring 90°. Therefore, the two acute angles must sum to:
$$180^{\circ} - 90^{\circ} = 90^{\circ}$$
This means the two acute angles are complementary — they always add up to 90°.

steps.title: Calculate the other acute angle
steps.content:
One acute angle is 23°. Since the two acute angles must sum to 90°, the other acute angle is:
$$90^{\circ} - 23^{\circ} = 67^{\circ}$$
